channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: A California lawmaker wants to ban AI from children’s toys
item.description: A California lawmaker has introduced a first-in-the-nation bill meant to ban companies from embedding AI chatbot technology into toys designed for children.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Heilweil, Fast Company

item.title: Sam Altman wants to pay some lucky person $555,000 a year to look after OpenAI's AI - but obviously, all is not quite as it seems
item.description: OpenAI is hiring a new head to look after model abuse and safety for $555k plus equity, but it'll be high-stress.
item.dc:creator: Craig Hale, TechRadar

item.title: Deepseek says new method can train AI more efficiently and cheaply
item.description: Chinese AI company Deepseek has unveiled a new training method, Manifold-Constrained Hyper-Connections (mHC), which will make it possible to train large language models more efficiently and at lower cost, reports the South China Morning Post.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Back to the future: The most popular JavaScript stories and themes of 2025
item.description: Artificial intelligence and its promise to revolutionize programming—and possibly overthrow human sovereignty—is a central story of the post-Covid world. But for JavaScript developers, it is only one of the forces contending for center stage.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: For Apple in ’26, it’s time to take the ‘Snow Leopard’ approach to OS updates
item.description: If the current speculation is correct, Apple’s 2026 operating system releases will follow a familiar path: they’re more likely to mimic the macOS Snow Leopard pattern set in 2009 than deliver a raft of snazzy new features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to watch Red Eye season 2
item.description: Hana Li (Jing Lusi) has no option but to navigate a political incident with Martin Compston's spiky head of security Clay. Here's how to watch Red Eye season 2 online.
item.dc:creator: Aatif Sulleyman, TechRadar

item.title: A fresh Samsung Galaxy S26 leak points to a launch date
item.description: While the Galaxy S26 phones might be launching later in the year, it sounds as though they won't cost any more.
item.dc:creator: David Nield, TechRadar

item.title: The top 10 AI stories of 2025
item.description: It’s safe to say that AI was the story of 2025: It topped CIOs’ tech agendas; vendors rolled out a constant stream of new AI products and features; and IT professionals and other knowledge workers got down to the business of integrating it into their workflows.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Nvidia licenses Groq’s inferencing chip tech and hires its leaders
item.description: Nvidia has licensed intellectual property from inferencing chip designer Groq, and hired away some of its senior executives, but stopped short of an outright acquisition.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Meta buys high-profile AI startup Manus
item.description: Meta is now buying the hyped AI startup Manus for about $2 billion, TechCrunch reported. Founded in Singapore by former Chinese entrepreneurs, Manus gained widespread attention after a viral demo video showing an AI agent that could handle everything from job interviews to stock analysis.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Top Intel stories of 2025
item.description: It’s been a roller coaster of a year for Intel, marked by the arrival of a new chief, new Xeon processors, select product divestitures, and sizable layoffs.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Using DISM to install Windows updates
item.description: Windows’ built-in Deployment Image Servicing and Management (DISM) command, a.k.a. dism.exe, is something of a Swiss Army knife when it comes to working on Windows OS images.
item.dc:creator: Computer World
